key_insight: A hybrid UNet-Transformer can act as a fast surrogate for FEM, predicting stress and strain fields directly from composite geometry images with high accuracy across most microstructure types.
engineering_relevance: This enables rapid, physics-informed evaluation of composite designs, reducing the cost of iterative material optimization and screening.
method: Input composite microstructure images to a hybrid UNet encoder-decoder with Transformer attention.
Predict 10 stress/strain field types across square, hexagonal, and triangular tessellations and varied boundary conditions.
Use Grad-CAM/Grad-CAM++ to inspect whether attention aligns with mechanically critical regions.
evidence_signal: Across most subdatasets the model performs strongly, with peak reported metrics including R2=0.9991, SSIM=0.9936, and MAE=0.0050, while six of eight subdatasets keep MAE below 0.05 on the normalized [0,1] scale.
watch_item: Performance drops on irregular square-grid geometries with sparse soft-phase inclusions, indicating difficulty capturing sharp stress discontinuities.
paper_title: Hybrid Unet-Transformer Model for Generating Stress and Strain Fields from Composite Geometrics
